subject: Fw: To Mr. Jeffrey Epstein, c/o Ms Groff
body:
want to go?

----- Forwarded Message ----
From: Lesley Groff <lesley@nysgllc.com>
To: jeeproject@yahoo.com
Sent: Wednesday, December 27, 2006 2:16:29 PM
Subject: FW: To Mr. Jeffrey Epstein, c/o Ms Groff

To Mr. Jeffrey Epstein, c/o Ms Groff

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Lesley Groff

Executive Assistant to Jeffrey 
Epstein

 

 




From: Tramo, Mark Jude 
[mailto:mark_tramo@hms.harvard.edu] 
Sent: Wednesday, December 27, 
2006 10:26 AM
To: Lesley Groff
Subject: To Mr. Jeffrey 
Epstein, c/o Ms Groff





Dear Jeffrey,

Season's Greetings!

1.  Thank 
you for bringing Martin Nowak and me together - he is a huge musicophile!  
I thought you would enjoy the email exchange below.  We are beginning to 
explore a joint project on music, grammar, evolution, and the 
brain.

2.  On Jan 8th 2P, The Institute for Music & Brain 
Science Board is meeting in Cambridge and, after the meeting, we are getting 
together with Scott Jones (Gracenote etc), whom Jack Gill (Vanguard Ventures) 
has identified as a potential fellow Board member who could help us R&D the 
"MusicMedPak" and "MusicHealthPak" I told you about (menuing systems will be 
important for patient-controlled sound environments and virtual 
realities).  The ffing day, Scott will lecture in Jack's MIT course on 
Entrepreneurship in the BioTech Industry.  The 1/8/07 mtg is for Board 
members with business acumen:  Jamie Singleton MBA (Cypress/Westco), 
Caroline Bienstock JD MBA (CarlinAmerica Music), Robbie Lee (I & Ear 
Records, Tom's son), Nick Zervas (HMS Neurosurg Chief/BSO ex-Pres), and Robert 
Freeman (long-time Director of the Eastman School of Music and ex-President of 
the New England Conservatory of Music, who retired and is interested in taking 
on the job of CEO for 6-12 mos to help build The Institute.)  Will you be 
able to attend?

Peace & Love,
Mark

Mark Jude Tramo, MD, 
PhD
Director, The Institute for Music & Brain Science
Dept of 
Neurology, Harvard Medical School & Massachusetts General 
Hospital
Steering Committee, Harvard University Mind/Brain/Behavior 
Interfaculty Initiative
America’s Top Doctors®/Best Doctors in America®/Best 
of Boston®
Songwriter Member, ASCAP

Email  
MTramo@HMS.Harvard.edu
URL  http://www.BrainMusic.org
Cellphone 
(24/7)  781-718-6390
Fax 781-990-1397
Page Operator (for call 
back)  617-726-2066



-----Original 
Message-----
From:   Tramo, Mark Jude
Sent:   Wed 
12/27/2006 10:08 AM
To:     Martin 
Nowak
Cc:    
Subject:        
To Marti w/ Congratulations/Thanks/Invitation

Dear Martin,

Thank 
you and CONGRATULATIONS on the laudatory review of your book in Nature (I am 
heading to Amazon after this email) and for the pdf of your review article in 
Science this month.  AND the Nature review.  Wow!

Please excuse 
the delay in writing this thank-you note amidst holidaying...let's get together 
and talk about Don Carlo etc - sounds like you experienced some autonomic 
overflow in Vienna! Might you be available for lunch (my turn) tomorrow Thurs 
Dec 28th?  I will be at Neurology Grand Rounds at MGH in the morning for 
our annual "Ether Drum" - started by a couple of drummer chief-residents 5 yrs 
ago - faculty, residents, students, and staff come to the Ether Dome at 9A to 
sit in a circle and drum for 45 mins together - I usually bring my Roland 808 
electronic kit...you are welcome to join us!

Holiday/New Year 
Cheers,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu





-----Original 
Message-----
From:   Martin Nowak [mailto:nowak@fas.harvard.edu]
Sent:   
Thu 12/21/2006 8:30 AM
To:     Tramo, Mark 
Jude
Cc:    
Subject:       


dear 
mark,
it was great to meet you.
i visited the vienna opera recently and 
saw an amazing performance of 
don carlo.
please find attached my 
most recent paper on cooperation, a review of 
my new book
and a 
paper on language learning and evolution.
best 
wishes
martin












__________________________________________________
Do You Yahoo!?
Tired of spam?  Yahoo! Mail has the best spam protection around 
http://mail.yahoo.com

Dear Jeffrey,

Season's Greetings!

1.  Thank you for bringing Martin Nowak and me together - he is a huge musicophile!  I thought you would enjoy the email exchange below.  We are beginning to explore a joint project on music, grammar, evolution, and the brain.

2.  On Jan 8th 2P, The Institute for Music & Brain Science Board is meeting in Cambridge and, after the meeting, we are getting together with Scott Jones (Gracenote etc), whom Jack Gill (Vanguard Ventures) has identified as a potential fellow Board member who could help us R&D the "MusicMedPak" and "MusicHealthPak" I told you about (menuing systems will be important for patient-controlled sound environments and virtual realities).  The ffing day, Scott will lecture in Jack's MIT course on Entrepreneurship in the BioTech Industry.  The 1/8/07 mtg is for Board members with business acumen:  Jamie Singleton MBA (Cypress/Westco), Caroline Bienstock JD MBA (CarlinAmerica Music), Robbie Lee (I & Ear Records, Tom's son), Nick Zervas (HMS Neurosurg Chief/BSO ex-Pres), and Robert Freeman (long-time Director of the Eastman School of Music and ex-President of the New England Conservatory of Music, who retired and is interested in taking on the job of CEO for 6-12 mos to help build The Institute.)  Will you be able to attend?

Peace & Love,
Mark

Subject:        To Marti w/ Congratulations/Thanks/Invitation

Dear Martin,

Thank you and CONGRATULATIONS on the laudatory review of your book in Nature (I am heading to Amazon after this email) and for the pdf of your review article in Science this month.  AND the Nature review.  Wow!

Please excuse the delay in writing this thank-you note amidst holidaying...let's get together and talk about Don Carlo etc - sounds like you experienced some autonomic overflow in Vienna! Might you be available for lunch (my turn) tomorrow Thurs Dec 28th?  I will be at Neurology Grand Rounds at MGH in the morning for our annual "Ether Drum" - started by a couple of drummer chief-residents 5 yrs ago - faculty, residents, students, and staff come to the Ether Dome at 9A to sit in a circle and drum for 45 mins together - I usually bring my Roland 808 electronic kit...you are welcome to join us!

Holiday/New Year Cheers,
Mark
mtramo@hms.harvard.edu
